data.text: I found the package, and the first pass suggests most of these are either test hooks or server integration points already used indirectly. I’m now checking the actual production wiring in `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the surrounding GraphQL helpers to separate true dead code from code that the static reachability pass can’t see through interface boundaries.

data.output:
1	// (LAYER 1) is no longer needed because resolver methods read WorkingDir
     2	// from the request context rather than a struct field.
     3	func (s *Server) serveGraphQL(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, workingDir string) {
     4		gqlServer := s.graphqlHandler()
     5		// Plumb the originating *http.Request into the resolver context so
     6		// resolvers (e.g. operatorPromptSubmit) can validate the CSRF header
     7		// and capture the originating identity from headers tsnetMiddleware
     8		// injects (X-Tailscale-User / X-Tailscale-Node). Also inject the
     9		// per-request workingDir so resolvers reading WorkingDir do so under
    10		// the scoped project (LAYER 2 / ddx-055e8d32) rather than the
    11		// resolver's startup default.
    12		ctx := r.Context()
    13		ctx = ddxgraphql.WithHTTPRequest(ctx, r)
    14		ctx = ddxgraphql.WithWorkingDir(ctx, workingDir)
    15		gqlServer.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    16	}
    17	
    18	// graphqlHandler lazily builds and caches the gqlgen handler used by both
    19	// /graphql and the scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route. The
    20	// resolver's WorkingDir field is set to s.WorkingDir as the FALLBACK
    21	// default; per-request scoping flows through context via WithWorkingDir
    22	// (see serveGraphQL).
    23	func (s *Server) graphqlHandler() http.Handler {
    24		s.gqlOnce.Do(func() {
    25			var fedProvider ddxgraphql.FederationProvider
    26			if s.hub != nil {
    27				fedProvider = newHubFederationProvider(s)
    28			}
    29			gqlServer := handler.New(ddxgraphql.NewExecutableSchema(ddxgraphql.Config{
    30				Resolvers: &ddxgraphql.Resolver{
    31					State:                              s.state,
    32					WorkingDir:                         s.WorkingDir,
    33					Workers:                            s.workers,
    34					BeadBus:                            s.beadHub,
    35					Actions:                            &workerDispatchAdapter{manager: s.workers},
    36					ExecLogs:                           &execLogAdapter{},
    37					CoordMetrics:                       &coordMetricsAdapter{reg: s.workers.LandCoordinators},
    38					CSRFTokens:                         s.csrfTokens,
    39					OperatorPromptIdempotency:          s.operatorPromptIdempotency,
    40					OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist: s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist,
    41					PromptCapBytes:                     serverPromptCapBytes,
    42					BuildSHA:                           serverBuildSHA(),
    43					NodeID:                             s.state.Node.ID,
    44					ExecuteLoopWaker:                   s.workers,
    45					Federation:                         fedProvider,
    46					ReportedWorkers:                    s.reportedWorkers,
    47				},
    48				Directives: ddxgraphql.DirectiveRoot{},
    49			}))
    50			gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
    51			gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
    52			gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.Websocket{
    53				Upgrader: websocket.Upgrader{
    54					CheckOrigin: func(r *http.Request) bool { return true },
    55				},
    56				KeepAlivePingInterval: 30 * time.Second,
    57			})
    58			s.gqlHandler = gqlServer
    59		})
    60		return s.gqlHandler
    61	}
    62	
    63	func (s *Server) handleGraphiQL(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    64		if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
    65			writeJSON(w, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, map[string]string{"error": "method not allowed"})
    66			return
    67		}
    68	
    69		// Serve GraphiQL HTML page with inline GraphQL endpoint reference
    70		graphiqlHTML := `<!DOCTYPE html>
    71	<html>
    72	<head>
    73		<meta charset="utf-8">
    74		<title>GraphiQL - DDx</title>
    75		<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/graphiql@0.18.0/graphiql.min.css" />
    76		<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/subscriptions-transport-ws@0.11.0/browser/client.js"></script>

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T151142-ee67431d/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T151142-ee67431d/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T151142-ee67431d/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T151142-ee67431d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T151142-ee67431d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.
